No Poverty.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to poverty eradication or related targets.

Zero Hunger.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to hunger, food security, or sustainable agriculture.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to health, well-being, or related medical/public health topics.

Quality Education.reason: The course is a university elective, directly contributing to tertiary education (4.3) and professional skills (4.4). It also covers sustainability issues (4.7). Model B's reasoning is more comprehensive.
evidence: 奠定會計專業知識與能力 (Students will gain professional accounting knowledge in their field.)
理解與辨別道德、社會責任與永續議題的能力 (Students will understand and be able to identify ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability issues.)
為未來 攻讀研究所課程奠定基礎

Gender Equality.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to gender equality or women's empowerment.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to water, sanitation, or hygiene.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: Model B's inferred link through '碳會計' (Carbon Accounting) to investment in clean energy (7.a) is plausible, as carbon accounting tracks energy-related emissions data.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course prepares students for decent work (8.5) and strengthens financial institutions (8.10) through accounting/auditing standards and industry-specific discussions. Model B's reasoning is stronger.
evidence: 奠定會計專業知識與能力
保險業與一般金融業的審計議題與專業發展

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course's focus on '永續揭露準則' supports sustainable industries (9.4) and linking practice with academic research enhances scientific research (9.5). Model B correctly identified these links.
evidence: 將實務議題與學術研究連結
培養其對研究問題之敏感度與分析能力

Reduced Inequalities.reason: Model B's inference that auditing standards enhance transparency and accountability, indirectly supporting improved financial market regulation (10.5), is reasonable.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to urban development, housing, or community sustainability.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: The course directly addresses Target 12.6 by teaching '永續揭露準則(IFRS S1/S2)' and ESG research, which focuses on integrating sustainability information into corporate reporting.
evidence: 理解與辨別...永續議題的能力 (Students will understand and be able to identify...sustainability issues.)

Climate Action.reason: The course's explicit coverage of '永續揭露準則與碳會計' (IFRS S2 for climate) directly contributes to integrating climate measures (13.2) and awareness (13.3). Model B's score is well-justified.
evidence: 永續揭露準則與碳會計 (Sustainability Disclosure Standards and Carbon Accounting)

Life Below Water.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to marine ecosystems or ocean health.

Life on Land.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to terrestrial ecosystems, biodiversity, or land degradation.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The study of auditing standards (TWSA 701, Key Audit Matters) directly promotes transparency, accountability, and good governance, supporting strong institutions (16.6) and reducing corruption (16.5).
evidence: 關鍵查核事項 (Key Audit Matters)

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course's focus on international accounting and sustainability standards (IFRS) enhances policy coherence (17.14) by providing common frameworks for global reporting and cooperation.
